title: ficus citrifolia var. brevifolia
intro: Ficus citrifolia var. brevifolia, also known as the Banyan Fig or Short-leaved Banyan Fig, is a variety of the Ficus citrifolia tree native to the Pacific Islands and Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ficus citrifolia var. brevifolia include treating fever, wounds, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide various health benefits.
